name: Alan Turing
key_contributions: Turing machine — the theoretical foundation of all computation
"Computing Machinery and Intelligence" (1950) — posed the question "Can machines think?" and proposed the Turing Test
Broke the Enigma code at Bletchley Park during WWII
Laid the philosophical and mathematical groundwork for artificial intelligence
summary: Alan Turing is the father of both computer science and artificial intelligence. His 1936 paper on computable numbers defined the theoretical limits of computation. His 1950 paper "Computing Machinery and Intelligence" launched the philosophical inquiry into machine thought and proposed the imitation game (Turing Test) as a benchmark. Without Turing, the field of AI would lack its foundational question and its first rigorous framework for thinking about machine intelligence.
